wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a professional B2B e-commerce platform for office consumables and stationery materials in Angola. Document the business objectives, target audience, and key value propositions. Describe the technology stack including Next.js 16, Convex real-time database, and modern frontend frameworks. Highlight the dual-facing architecture with public website and protected backoffice administration. Include the project's scope covering lead management, content management, and e-commerce functionality. Provide an architectural overview showing how the frontend React application integrates with the Convex backend services. Address the business impact and strategic importance of this digital presence for ADIKI ALVANIR Angola.

wiki_catalogs.name: Next.js Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Next.js application structure and routing system. Detail the App Router configuration and file-based routing conventions used throughout the application. Document the main layout hierarchy starting from the root layout.tsx through nested layouts and page components. Explain the routing patterns for different page types including dynamic routes, catch-all routes, and API routes. Document the metadata configuration including sitemap generation and robots.txt management. Describe the static generation strategies and server-side rendering approaches. Include the build configuration and optimization settings. Address the integration between client-side navigation, server-side rendering, and static generation. Explain the asset optimization and image handling strategies. Document the deployment configuration and production optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Public Content Delivery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the public content delivery system. Explain the dynamic content fetching architecture using Convex database queries and the fetchQuery function. Document the content structure including products, categories, blog posts, and media assets with their respective data models and transformations. Detail the fallback mechanisms when Convex is unavailable and the static content backup system. Explain the content merging process for images and the icon mapping system for categories. Document the error handling strategies and content validation processes. Include practical examples of content retrieval patterns and caching considerations. Address the integration between Convex backend services and frontend content display components. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common content delivery issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lead Capture & Submission Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the lead capture and submission workflow. Document the complete customer journey from contact form interaction through form validation to lead submission. Explain the frontend contact form implementation including field validation, user experience enhancements, and form state management. Detail the server-side lead processing including input sanitization, validation rules, and error handling mechanisms. Document the honeypot anti-spam technique and its implementation. Explain the form normalization processes for data cleaning and standardization. Cover the submission state management with success/error feedback mechanisms. Include the integration between frontend components and server actions. Address form accessibility features and mobile responsiveness. Document the user experience considerations throughout the lead capture process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and security documentation for the backoffice system. Detail the session-based authentication mechanism including cookie management, session expiration, and security measures. Document the password hashing implementation using scrypt algorithm and secure verification processes. Explain the session payload structure, signing mechanism, and timing-safe comparison techniques. Detail the login page implementation, form validation, and error handling. Document the environment variable requirements including BACKOFFICE_SESSION_SECRET, BACKOFFICE_PASSWORD_HASH, and BACKOFFICE_API_KEY configurations. Explain the session lifecycle from creation to expiration, including automatic renewal and cleanup processes. Detail the security measures including CSRF protection, secure cookie attributes, and HTTPS enforcement. Document the role-based access control with admin privileges and permission validation. Include troubleshooting guidance for authentication failures, session issues, and security-related problems. Provide security best practices for production deployments and credential management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Convex Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Convex database schema definition. Detail the complete schema structure including all five main collections: leads, mediaAssets, products, categories, and blogPosts. Document the defineSchema function usage and table definitions. Explain the schema validation system using convex/values and the v.* type definitions. Cover the index definitions for performance optimization including by_status, by_created_at, by_kind_and_status, by_active_and_sort_order, and by_slug indexes. Document the referential integrity patterns and foreign key relationships between collections. Include the siteSettings collection for configuration management. Provide examples of schema evolution patterns and migration strategies. Address the real-time database capabilities and automatic synchronization features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Convex Backend API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Convex backend services. Document all Convex functions including mutations and queries with detailed parameter schemas, return types, and usage examples. Explain the Convex function architecture, argument validation patterns, and database interaction methods. Detail the leads management API with create and recent query endpoints, including request/response formats and error handling. Document the backoffice authentication API and administrative functions. Explain the real-time database integration patterns and reactive data synchronization. Include the generated API types and TypeScript interfaces for type-safe client integration. Document the Convex deployment configuration and environment setup. Provide examples of client-side API consumption and integration patterns. Address performance considerations, query optimization, and indexing strategies used in the Convex schema.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the backoffice system. Detail the session-based authentication mechanism including password hashing with scrypt, session creation and validation, and secure cookie management. Document the login flow, session lifecycle management, and automatic session expiration. Explain the role-based access control with admin privileges and session verification patterns. Include the cryptographic implementation details for password hashing, HMAC signing, and timing-safe comparisons. Document the environment variable requirements including BACKOFFICE_SESSION_SECRET and BACKOFFICE_PASSWORD_HASH. Provide implementation examples for login handlers, session validation middleware, and protected route guards. Address security considerations for cookie attributes, HTTPS enforcement, and session security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend performance optimization documentation for the Next.js application. Detail the implementation of Next.js Image Optimization including automatic image resizing, format conversion, and lazy loading strategies. Document the revalidation configuration (60-second cache) and its impact on performance. Explain the use of priority images for above-the-fold content and the strategic placement of critical rendering paths. Detail the component-level optimizations including the Reveal animation system for progressive enhancement. Document the Tailwind CSS optimization strategies including unused CSS purging and custom property usage for reduced bundle size. Explain the code splitting implementation through dynamic imports and route-based splitting. Include performance monitoring techniques using Next.js built-in metrics and Lighthouse analysis. Address Core Web Vitals optimization including Largest Contentful Paint (LCP), First Input Delay (FID), and Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS). Provide specific examples from the homepage implementation showing optimized image loading, component lazy loading, and efficient data fetching patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vercel Deployment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Vercel deployment documentation for the Next.js application. Detail the Vercel platform configuration including project setup, environment variables, build settings, and deployment triggers. Document the deployment pipeline from local development to production including branch-based deployments and preview deployments. Explain the static site generation configuration and serverless function deployment. Detail the custom domain setup, SSL certificate management, and CDN configuration. Include the build optimization settings, image optimization, and performance monitoring. Document the deployment rollback procedures and version management. Address common deployment issues such as build failures, environment variable conflicts, and performance bottlenecks. Provide troubleshooting guidance for Vercel-specific problems and deployment best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed unit testing documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website. Explain the unit testing approach for React components including component testing with React Testing Library, hook testing for custom hooks, and utility function validation. Document the testing strategies for server actions, form validation logic, and data transformation functions. Detail the mock strategies for Convex API calls, database interactions, and external dependencies. Include concrete examples of testing component props, state changes, and user interactions. Explain testing patterns for asynchronous operations, error handling scenarios, and edge cases. Document the Jest configuration, test file organization, and naming conventions. Address testing best practices for TypeScript components, prop validation, and component lifecycle testing. Provide guidance for writing maintainable unit tests and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, development tools setup, and environment preparation. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for cloning the repository, installing dependencies using npm install, and configuring the development environment. Document the local development setup including running 'npm run dev' for Next.js development server and 'npm run convex:dev' for Convex backend services. Explain the project structure and key directories. Include initial configuration steps for Convex development environment and local database setup. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Address environment variable configuration and security considerations for local development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the component architecture and composition patterns. Document the hierarchical organization of components starting from the main site-chrome.tsx wrapper through specialized site components like navbar and footer. Explain the component composition patterns including container components, presentational components, and higher-order components. Detail the reusable UI component library including button, input, and textarea components with their props, variants, and customization options. Document the component lifecycle management and prop passing strategies. Explain the component state management patterns including local state, context usage, and prop drilling avoidance. Address the component testing strategies and storybook integration. Include the component performance optimization techniques and lazy loading patterns. Document the accessibility implementation and ARIA attributes usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the product management system. Explain the product data model including name, category, description, and image fields. Document the product catalog component architecture and how products are displayed with filtering and sorting capabilities. Detail the product creation, editing, and deletion workflows in the backoffice interface. Explain the product-image association system and fallback mechanisms for missing images. Document the product-category relationships and how categories influence product organization. Include the product validation rules and sanitization processes. Address the product search functionality and filtering options. Provide examples of product data structures and component usage patterns. Document the integration between product management and the public content delivery system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Lead Processing & Storage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend documentation for lead processing and database storage. Detail the Convex backend functions for lead creation, validation, and data persistence. Document the lead schema structure including field definitions, data types, and validation constraints. Explain the database indexing strategy for optimal query performance and lead retrieval. Document the lead creation mutation function including parameter validation, data transformation, and error handling. Detail the data normalization processes applied during lead storage. Explain the integration with Convex Storage for any attached media or documents. Cover the database query patterns for lead retrieval, filtering, and aggregation. Document the performance optimization techniques used in lead processing. Address data consistency and transaction handling. Include troubleshooting guidance for common backend processing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the backoffice dashboard and analytics system. Detail the administrative navigation patterns and quick access features for common tasks. Include the integration between dashboard components and administrative workflows. Provide guidance on customizing dashboard widgets and adding new analytics features. Address performance optimization for large datasets and efficient data loading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lead Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Lead data model used in customer inquiry management. Include integration patterns with the contact form and lead management interface.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Next.js server actions and form processing implementation. Address the security implications of different input types and the measures taken to ensure data integrity.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed database query and schema optimization documentation for the Convex backend services. Detail the use of Convex indexes for improved query performance and explain the trade-offs between read and write performance. Document the data access patterns used throughout the application including batching queries, avoiding N+1 problems, and implementing proper caching strategies. Explain the relationship between schema design and query performance, including denormalization strategies where appropriate. Detail the Convex function optimization techniques including proper use of server actions, efficient data serialization, and minimizing network round trips. Include performance monitoring approaches for database queries and identify common performance bottlenecks. Provide specific examples from the public content fetching implementation showing optimized query patterns and data structure design decisions.

Explain the image fallback mechanisms and default asset management. Document the media asset validation rules and file type restrictions. Include the media asset preview and thumbnail generation processes. Address the media asset cleanup and orphaned file management. Provide examples of media asset data structures and upload workflows. Document the integration between media management and content creation interfaces for products, categories, and blog posts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blog Post Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Blog Post data model used in content management. Document the complete blog post structure including title, slug, excerpt, body content, category, and read time fields. Explain the media asset relationship for featured images and fallback handling. Detail the published status flag and publication timestamp for content scheduling. Document the timestamp fields (createdAt, updatedAt) and their role in content management workflows. Explain the indexing strategy for published posts and slug-based lookups. Include validation rules for post attributes and content formatting. Provide examples of blog post CRUD operations, content publishing workflows, and integration with the blog interface. Address content lifecycle management and SEO optimization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content management system documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website. Explain the dynamic content fetching architecture using Convex database queries and server actions. Document the content types including products, categories, blog posts, and media assets with their respective management interfaces. Detail the content rendering pipeline from database queries through component composition to final presentation. Explain the content validation and sanitization processes for user-generated content. Document the caching strategies for improved performance and content delivery optimization. Address the content workflow including creation, editing, approval processes, and publication scheduling. Include the integration between Convex backend services and frontend content display components. Explain the content organization patterns, search functionality, and filtering mechanisms. Document the content management interfaces for different user roles and permission levels. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common content management issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Asset Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Media Asset data model used for image and file management. Document the complete media asset structure including storageId, filename, alt text, kind classifications, content type, and file size. Explain the kind enum values (hero, product, category, blog, logo, general) and their usage contexts. Detail the status management (active, archived) and upload timestamp tracking. Document the indexing strategy for kind and status combinations. Include validation rules for file metadata and storage integration patterns. Provide examples of media upload workflows, asset reference patterns, and integration with other collections. Address media optimization, storage considerations, and cleanup procedures for archived assets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lead Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive lead management system documentation for customer inquiry capture and processing. Detail the lead submission workflow from contact form through server actions to database storage. Document the lead validation and sanitization processes including input validation, spam prevention, and data quality assurance. Explain the lead status management system with automated workflows and manual intervention capabilities. Detail the administrative lead management interface including filtering, sorting, and bulk operations. Document the lead analytics and reporting features for tracking conversion rates and customer acquisition metrics. Include the integration between frontend contact forms and backend lead processing functions. Explain the notification systems for lead alerts and follow-up workflows. Address lead data privacy and compliance considerations including GDPR requirements and data retention policies. Document the lead scoring and qualification processes for sales team integration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common lead processing issues and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Site Settings Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Site Settings data model used for configuration management. Document the complete settings structure including key-value pairs and update timestamp tracking. Explain the indexing strategy for key-based lookups and configuration retrieval patterns. Detail the validation rules for setting keys and values. Include examples of common configuration scenarios such as contact information, social media links, and operational settings. Provide patterns for settings retrieval, caching strategies, and dynamic configuration updates. Address settings versioning, backup procedures, and integration with the public content system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backoffice Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backoffice administration documentation for the protected administrative interface. Document the session-based authentication system including login mechanisms, session management, and security measures. Detail the administrative dashboard with lead statistics, content inventory, and system overview features. Explain the role-based access control and permission management for different administrative users. Document the administrative workflows for content creation, modification, and deletion across all content types. Detail the media asset management system including upload, organization, and metadata management. Explain the administrative reporting and analytics features for system monitoring and performance tracking. Include the integration between administrative interfaces and Convex backend services. Document the administrative security measures including input validation, CSRF protection, and audit logging. Address the administrative user experience including navigation patterns, form handling, and error management. Provide troubleshooting guidance for administrative access issues and system maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration & Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API integration documentation for the Convex backend services and external integrations. Document the Convex API endpoints including function definitions, parameter schemas, and response formats. Detail the server action implementation patterns for secure data processing and form handling. Explain the real-time data synchronization capabilities and reactive programming patterns. Document the external service integrations including WhatsApp API for customer communication, Google Maps for location services, and social media connectivity. Detail the API authentication mechanisms and security considerations for external service access. Include the integration patterns for media asset management through Convex Storage. Explain the webhook implementations and event-driven architectures where applicable. Document the API error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and monitoring approaches. Address the API versioning strategy and backward compatibility considerations. Provide integration examples and troubleshooting guidance for common API integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security implementation documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website. Detail the session-based authentication system including login security, session management, and credential handling. Document the input validation and sanitization processes for preventing injection attacks and data corruption. Explain the CSRF protection mechanisms and form security measures. Detail the authorization patterns and role-based access control for administrative interfaces. Document the environment variable management and secret handling for production deployments. Include the security measures for file uploads, media asset management, and content validation. Explain the rate limiting and abuse prevention mechanisms. Detail the audit logging and security monitoring capabilities. Address the data encryption strategies for sensitive information and secure data transmission. Document the security testing approaches and vulnerability assessment procedures. Provide incident response procedures and security update management processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive performance optimization documentation for the Next.js application and Convex backend services. Detail the frontend performance optimization strategies including image optimization, lazy loading implementation, and bundle size reduction. Document the server-side rendering optimization techniques and static generation strategies. Explain the database query optimization including index usage, query patterns, and caching strategies. Detail the Convex function optimization for improved backend performance and reduced latency. Include the performance monitoring and analytics implementation for tracking application metrics. Document the CDN integration and content delivery optimization strategies. Explain the code splitting and dynamic imports for improved loading performance. Detail the memory management and resource optimization techniques. Address the performance testing methodologies and benchmarking approaches. Provide performance troubleshooting guidance and optimization best practices for different deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website. Detail the Vercel deployment configuration including environment variables, build settings, and deployment pipelines. Document the Convex deployment process including database migrations, function deployment, and production environment setup. Explain the CI/CD pipeline configuration for automated testing and deployment workflows. Detail the environment management including development, staging, and production environments with their specific configurations. Include the monitoring and alerting setup for production systems. Document the backup and disaster recovery procedures for both frontend and backend components. Explain the scaling strategies for handling traffic spikes and increased demand. Detail the security considerations for production deployments including SSL certificates and security headers. Address the rollback procedures and emergency response protocols. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues and performance monitoring in production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website. Detail the unit testing approach for React components including component testing, hook testing, and utility function validation. Document the integration testing strategies for API endpoints, database interactions, and server action validation. Explain the end-to-end testing procedures for critical user workflows including lead submission and content management. Detail the testing framework setup including Jest configuration, React Testing Library usage, and mock strategies. Include the testing best practices for asynchronous operations, form validation, and error handling scenarios. Document the performance testing approaches for load testing and stress testing scenarios. Explain the accessibility testing procedures and cross-browser compatibility testing. Detail the continuous integration testing pipeline and automated quality gates. Address the testing documentation standards and test case management procedures. Provide guidance for writing effective tests and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing guidelines documentation for the ADIKI ALVANIR Angola website project. Detail the development workflow including branch management, commit message standards, and pull request procedures. Document the code style guidelines using ESLint configuration and TypeScript best practices. Explain the review process including code review checklists, quality standards, and approval workflows. Detail the documentation requirements for new features, API changes, and architectural decisions. Include the testing requirements for code contributions and regression testing procedures. Document the environment setup for contributors including local development configuration and dependency management. Explain the communication channels and collaboration practices for team coordination. Detail the issue reporting procedures and bug fix workflows. Address the release management process and versioning strategies. Provide guidance for onboarding new contributors and maintaining code quality standards.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ADIKI ALVANIR Angola Website - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
ADIKI ALVANIR Angola is a professional B2B e-commerce platform specializing in office consumables and stationery materials for Angolan businesses. The website serves as a comprehensive digital solution for corporate procurement, offering premium office supplies, delivery services, and expert consultation for office management needs.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Primary Goal**: Establish ADIKI ALVANIR as the premier supplier of office consumables in Angola
- **Target Audience**: Companies, institutions, and teams requiring reliable office supply solutions
- **Value Proposition**: Streamlined procurement process with expert consultation, organized delivery, and quality assurance
- **Market Positioning**: Professional, trustworthy, and efficient office supply partner focused on business needs

### Business Model
The platform operates as a B2B marketplace with a focus on:
- Direct sales of office consumables
- Customized supply chain management
- Consultative approach to procurement
- Strategic partnerships with local businesses

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js Frontend] --> B[React Components]
    A --> C[Convex Backend]
    
    B --> D[Site Components]
    B --> E[Backoffice Components]
    
    D --> F[UI Library]
    D --> G[Content Management]
    D --> H[Navigation]
    
    C --> I[Database Schema]
    C --> J[Storage Management]
    C --> K[API Endpoints]
    
    I --> L[Leads Collection]
    I --> M[Products Collection]
    I --> N[Categories Collection]
    I --> O[Blog Posts Collection]
    I --> P[Media Assets Collection]
    
    J --> Q[Image Storage]
    K --> R[Public Content API]
    K --> S[Admin APIs]
```

### Design Patterns

**1. Component-Based Architecture**
- Modular React components with clear separation of concerns
- Shared UI components across different pages
- Consistent design system with custom theming

**2. Data Flow Pattern**
- Static content from `site-data.ts` for consistent branding
- Dynamic content fetched via Convex database queries
- Server-side rendering with client-side hydration

**3. Authentication Pattern**
- Session-based authentication for backoffice access
- Environment variable security for admin credentials
- Role-based access control for content management

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Next.js
    participant Convex
    participant Database
    
    User->>Next.js: Request Homepage
    Next.js->>Convex: Fetch Public Content
    Convex->>Database: Query Products/Categories
    Database-->>Convex: Return Data
    Convex-->>Next.js: Processed Content
    Next.js-->>User: Rendered Page
    
    User->>Next.js: Submit Contact Form
    Next.js->>Convex: Create Lead
    Convex->>Database: Insert Lead Record
    Database-->>Convex: Confirm Insertion
    Convex-->>Next.js: Success Response
    Next.js-->>User: Confirmation Message
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Layout**: `app/layout.tsx` - Global layout with metadata and SEO optimization
- **Homepage**: `app/page.tsx` - Hero section, features, testimonials, and call-to-action
- **Backoffice Layout**: `app/backoffice/(admin)/layout.tsx` - Admin panel with authentication

### Core Modules

**1. Content Management System**
- **Site Data**: `lib/site-data.ts` - Centralized content configuration
- **Public Content**: `lib/public-content.ts` - Dynamic content fetching from Convex
- **Data Types**: Strongly typed interfaces for content consistency

**2. Database Schema**
- **Convex Schema**: `convex/schema.ts` - Structured data model with indexes
- **Collections**: Leads, Products, Categories, Blog Posts, Media Assets
- **Indexes**: Optimized queries for common operations

**3. Authentication System**
- **Backoffice Auth**: `lib/backoffice-auth.ts` - Session management
- **Admin UI**: `components/backoffice/admin-ui.tsx` - Administrative interface components

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: `package.json` scripts for development and deployment
- **Tailwind CSS**: `app/globals.css` - Custom design system with brand colors
- **TypeScript**: Strict typing throughout the application

### External Dependencies
- **Next.js 16**: Modern React framework with App Router
- **Convex**: Real-time database and backend services
- **Framer Motion**: Smooth animations and transitions
- **Lucide React**: Consistent iconography
- **Radix UI**: Accessible UI primitives

### Integration Points

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Frontend] --> B[Convex API]
    B --> C[Database]
    B --> D[Storage]
    B --> E[Authentication]
    
    F[WhatsApp] --> G[External Integration]
    H[Google Maps] --> I[Location Services]
    J[Analytics] --> K[Performance Monitoring]
```

**1. Convex Integration**: Real-time database with automatic indexing
**2. Image Storage**: Convex Storage for media assets with upload URLs
**3. Social Integration**: WhatsApp direct messaging
**4. SEO Optimization**: Open Graph and Twitter Card metadata

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[SiteChrome] --> B[Navbar]
    A --> C[Footer]
    A --> D[WhatsApp Float]
    
    E[HomePage] --> F[Hero Section]
    E --> G[Features]
    E --> H[Testimonials]
    E --> I[Services]
    
    F --> J[CTA Buttons]
    G --> K[Advantages Cards]
    H --> L[Partner Logos]
    I --> M[Service Cards]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**1. Customer Experience**
- Responsive design optimized for Angolan users
- Portuguese language interface with local cultural context
- Mobile-first approach for field workers and managers
- Fast loading times with optimized image delivery

**2. Business Operations**
- Lead management system for customer inquiries
- Product catalog with categorization
- Content management for blog articles
- Media asset management for images

**3. Administrative Features**
- Secure backoffice with role-based access
- Real-time lead tracking and status updates
- Content editing capabilities
- Media upload and organization

### Implementation Highlights

**1. Performance Optimization**
- `app/page.tsx` - Uses `revalidate` for controlled caching
- Lazy loading for images and components
- Optimized font loading with `next/font/google`
- Efficient data fetching with Convex

**2. Accessibility Features**
- Semantic HTML structure
- Proper ARIA labels for interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation support
- Reduced motion preferences handling

**3. Security Measures**
- Environment variable protection
- Input sanitization and validation
- CSRF protection for forms
- Rate limiting for contact submissions

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> ContentReady: Data Fetched
    ContentReady --> UserInteraction: Page Rendered
    UserInteraction --> FormSubmission: Contact Form
    FormSubmission --> Validation: Input Check
    Validation --> Success: Valid Data
    Validation --> Error: Invalid Data
    Success --> LeadCreated: Convex API Call
    Error --> UserInteraction: Show Error
    LeadCreated --> [*]: Success Response
```

**1. Content Delivery Pipeline**
- Static content from `site-data.ts`
- Dynamic content from Convex database
- Fallback mechanisms for offline scenarios
- Image optimization and lazy loading

**2. User Journey Management**
- Navigation flow from homepage to contact
- Lead capture and processing
- Status updates and notifications
- Follow-up communication channels

**3. Administrative Workflow**
- Login authentication
- Content management interface
- Media asset organization
- Publication workflows

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [site-data.ts](lib/site-data.ts)
- [site-chrome.tsx](components/site/site-chrome.tsx)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [schema.ts](convex/schema.ts)
- [public-content.ts](lib/public-content.ts)
- [layout.tsx](app/backoffice/(admin)/layout.tsx)
- [admin-ui.tsx](components/backoffice/admin-ui.tsx)
- [button.tsx](components/ui/button.tsx)
- [globals.css](app/globals.css)
- [backoffice.ts](convex/backoffice.ts)
- [lead-actions.ts](app/actions/lead-actions.ts)
- [BACKOFFICE.md](docs/BACKOFFICE.md)

</blog>
